summary: Dan Shapiro recounts accidentally cutting his thumb with a box knife while trimming picture frame splines, resulting in a trip to the ER and five stitches. The incident happened because he was using a knife instead of the proper flush cut saw he'd been too cheap to buy. He shares three lessons learned: always consider where the knife will go if it slips, invest in the right tools instead of improvising, and bring a book to the ER. He publicly shares the embarrassing story hoping it will make him more careful in the future.
key_points: Using the wrong tool because of cost savings led to an avoidable injury
Always anticipate where a cutting tool will go if the workpiece gives way suddenly
Public accountability through blogging about mistakes as a strategy for self-improvement
money_quotes: Don't skimp; buy the right tools for the job.
Bring a book if you have to go to the ER.
Make sure that if the work gives suddenly, the knife doesn't hit you.
One week ago I was considering buying a flush cut saw and decided not to because I'm a cheapskate.
Maybe if I embarass myself publically on my blog, I'll be more careful next time.
key_insight: Skimping on the right tools is a false economy that can cost you far more than what you saved.
